item.title: Ardie Savea to captain All Blacks, Ruben Love forces his way into the squad
item.description: Ardie Savea will captain the All Blacks after head coach Dave Rennie named a 34-man squad for the opening three Tests of the 2026 season, with Hurricanes first five-eighth Ruben Love earning selection and prop George Bower, who is of Fijian descent, among the forwards included. The squad features a blend of experienced internationals and emerging talent, with key names such as Beauden Barrett, Dam
item.content:encoded:
Ardie Savea will captain the All Blacks after head coach Dave Rennie named a 34-man squad for the opening three Tests of the 2026 season, with Hurricanes first five-eighth Ruben Love earning selection and prop George Bower, who is of Fijian descent, among the forwards included.

The squad features a blend of experienced internationals and emerging talent, with key names such as Beauden Barrett, Damian McKenzie, Will Jordan and Jordie Barrett all retained.

In the forwards, hookers Asafo Aumua, Codie Taylor and Samisoni Taukei’aho have been selected, while joining Bower in the front row are Ethan de Groot, Tyrel Lomax, Fletcher Newell, Pasilio Tosi and uncapped Xavier Numia.

The lock group is made up of Tupou Vaa’i, Patrick Tuipulotu, Josh Lord and Sam Darry.

In the loose forwards, Savea leads a unit featuring Wallace Sititi, Luke Jacobson, Peter Lakai, Simon Parker and uncapped Anton Segner.

At halfback, Cameron Roigard, Cortez Ratima and Kyle Preston have been named, while the first five-eighths options are Ruben Love, Beauden Barrett and Damian McKenzie.

The midfield includes Jordie Barrett, Quinn Tupaea, Billy Proctor and Anton Lienert-Brown, while the outside backs selected are Caleb Clarke, Will Jordan, Leroy Carter, Josh Moorby and uncapped Fehi Fineanganofo.

The squad marks the start of Rennie’s 2026 campaign as the All Blacks prepare for their opening Test series.

item.title: Egypt into last 32 after 3-1 win over NZ
item.description: Egypt into last 32 after 3-1 win over NZ

item.title: Boginisoko signs with Fijian Drua for next season
item.description: The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua has announced the signing of Fiji Airways National Mens 7s representative Nacani Boginisoko to its main squad ahead of the 2027 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific season. Boginisoko, who played for the Nadi rugby team last year, joins the Drua after an impressive rise through Fijiân rugby ranks.
item.content:encoded:
The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua has announced the signing of Fiji Airways National Mens 7s representative Nacani Boginisoko to its main squad ahead of the 2027 Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific season.

Boginisoko, who played for the Nadi rugby team last year, joins the Drua after an impressive rise through Fijiân rugby ranks.

The dynamic outside back has also featured in eight HSBC SVNS tournaments for Fiji, scoring two tries and establishing himself as one of the countrys most exciting emerging talents.

The 26-year-old fromÂ Naivakarauniniu Village in Sanima, Kadavu,A first caught the attention of national selectors through his standout performances for Dominion Brothers in local sevens competitions.

Boginisoko earned his maiden call-up to the Fiji Airways Fiji Mens 7s squad for the Singapore and Perth tournaments and made his international debut in Singapore.

He went on to play a key role in Fijians campaign throughout the HSBC SVNS Series, continuing to impress with his athleticism and consistency.

With maternal links to Nadroga, Boginisoko expressed his excitement about joining the Drua.

Boginisoko says he was excited when he got the call to sign his contract with the Drua, and is grateful to the management for believing in him and giving him the opportunity to be part of the team for next season.

Boginisoko first made headlines as a standout performer for Dominion Brothers, earning the Under-21 Player of the Tournament award at the Marist 7s and cementing his reputation as one of Fijians brightest rugby prospects.

His signing further highlights the strong pathway from local competitions to professional rugby for Fijians homegrown talent.

Fijian Drua General Manager Rugby Baden Stephenson, welcomed the addition of the talented outside back.

He says Boginosoko is an exciting talent who has earned this opportunity through his performances and work ethic and has shown tremendous growth over the past few seasons, progressing from local competitions to representing Fiji on the international sevens stage.

Stephenson says Boginisokos arrival adds further depth and excitement to the Drua squad as preparations begin for the 2027 Super Rugby Pacific season.

item.title: NZ take 1-0 lead against Egypt in crucial World Cup clash
item.description: The All Whites are currently leading Egypt at half time of their second FIFA World Cup match. Finn Surman scored the goal in the 15th minute.
item.content:encoded:
The All Whites are currently leading Egypt at half time of their second FIFA World Cup match.

Finn Surman scored the goal in the 15th minute.

The winner of this match is likely to qualify for the last 32.

In other matches today, Cape Verde drew 2-all against Urugyay, Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-nil while Belgium and Iran finished the match in a nil-all draw.

item.title: Cape Verde earn historic 2-2 draw against Uruguay
item.description: Cape Verde has drawn 2-all against Uruguay in their second FIFA World Cup match. Kevin Pina fired Cape Verde into the lead in the 21st minute with an incredible direct free kick, scoring his nation's first ever goal at the World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Cape Verde has drawn 2-all against Uruguay in their second FIFA World Cup match.

Kevin Pina fired Cape Verde into the lead in the 21st minute with an incredible direct free kick, scoring his nation's first ever goal at the World Cup.

But Uruguay responded with two goals, from Maxi Araujo and Agustin Canobbio, in the closing minutes of the first half to turn the scoreline around.

Cape Verde then answered with an equaliser of their own just after the hour mark, as Helio Varela capitalised on disastrous defending.

In other matches, Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-nil, Belgium and Iran finished the match in a nil-all draw.

New Zealand will take on Egypt at 1pm. Source: New York Times

item.title: Reenie to name All Blacks squad at midday
item.description: All Blacks Dave Rennie will name the first squad of his tenure in Feilding this morning. Rennie, who will name 34 players for the three Nations Championship tests in New Zealand in July, will also name a new captain because incumbent Scott Barrett is injured.
item.content:encoded:
All Blacks Dave Rennie will name the first squad of his tenure in Feilding this morning.

Rennie, who will name 34 players for the three Nations Championship tests in New Zealand in July, will also name a new captain because incumbent Scott Barrett is injured.

The anticipation of the squad naming has been heightened by the prospect of Rennie and his fellow selectors Graham Henry and Neil Barnes blooding uncapped players for the tests against France, Italy and Ireland.

If Barrett had been fit, he was no certainty to be asked to continue as captain. A new coach often coincides with a new skipper being appointed.

Rennie, having recently returned from Japan where he guided the Kobe Kobelco Steelers to the League One title, has already surrounded himself with several new assistants.

Former All Blacks captain Tana Umaga will be in charge of defence, ex-Scotland international Mike Blair will organise the attack, and Barnes has been appointed senior assistant coach and lineout expert.

Jason Ryan, who has been with the All Blacks since mid-2022, is the forwards coach

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Ten-man Belgium hold Iran to goalless draw
item.description: Belgium and Iran played out a tense 0-0 draw in their FIFA World Cup Group G clash, with both sides unable to find a breakthrough despite creating several chances. Belgium were forced to play with ten men after defender Nathan Ngoy was shown a red card , leaving the Europeans to battle through much of the contest a player short.
item.content:encoded:
Belgium and Iran played out a tense 0-0 draw in their FIFA World Cup Group G clash, with both sides unable to find a breakthrough despite creating several chances.

Belgium were forced to play with ten men after defender Nathan Ngoy was shown a red card , leaving the Europeans to battle through much of the contest a player short.

Iran thought they had taken the lead through striker Mehdi Taremi following a well-worked free-kick routine, but the goal was ruled out for offside , much to the frustration of the Iranian side.

Despite the numerical disadvantage, Belgium defended resolutely to earn a valuable point and remain in contention in Group G.

The result follows Belgium's 1-1 draw against Egypt in their opening match, while Iran had previously shared the points in a 2-2 draw with New Zealand.

With both teams still searching for their first win of the tournament, the race for qualification from Group G remains wide open heading into the final round of group matches.

Earlier this morning, Spain thrashed Saudi Arabia 4-0.

Meanwhile, Uruguay will face Cabo Verde at 10am while New Zealand will play Egypt at 1pm.

item.title: Nicoll-Klokstad bags four tries as Warriors beat Cowboys in Christchurch
item.description: A packed and highly charged One NZ Stadium in Christchurch set the scene and the Warriors delivered, overpowering the Cowboys 38-2

item.description: McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s coach Mike Legge has acknowledged the determination and resilience shown by his side in their 42-25 w

item.title: Japan cruises past Tunisia 4-0 in dominant World Cup display
item.description: Japan strengthened its FIFA World Cup knockout hopes with a convincing 4-0 victory over Tunisia in their Group F clash. Daichi Kam
